title: Culturing method for cystic fibrosis cells and screening method based thereon and treatment of cystic fibrosis
abstract: The present invention relates to methods for screening cystic fibrosis patient cells to identify suitable therapeutic agents for the treatment of cystic fibrosis and/or the bacterial infections associated with cystic fibrosis. In particular, the present invention relates to a method of culturing cells from a cystic fibrosis patient comprising: (i) providing primary airway epithelial cells (pAEC) from said cystic fibrosis patient; and (ii) co-culturing said pAEC with an irradiated fibroblast feeder cell in the presence of tissue culture medium and at least one Rho-associated kinase (ROCK) inhibitor to expand the number of pAEC in culture. In addition, the invention relates to an expression construct comprising a nucleic sequence encoding a reporter polypeptide operatively linked to a nucleic acid sequence encoding CFTR or a mutant thereof, which facilitates expression of 150,000 copies of the CFTR or mutant CFTR polypeptide on the cytoplasmic membrane of a cell.
claims: Claims CLAIMS: 1. A method of culturing cells from a cystic fibrosis patient comprising: (i) providing primary airway epithelial cells (pAEC) from said cystic fibrosis patient; and (ii) co-culturing said pAEC with an irradiated fibroblast feeder cell in the presence of tissue culture medium and at least one Rho-associated kinase (ROCK) inhibitor to expand the number of pAEC in culture. 2. A method according to claim 1, further comprising the step of: (iii) recovering said pAEC following expansion in number. 3. A method according to claim 1 or claim 2, further comprising the step of serially passaging the recovered pAEC to produce expanded pAEC, wherein the expanded pAEC maintain their lineage during expansion. 4. A method according to claim 3, wherein the expanded pAEC preserve the phenotypic and functional abnormalities of the pAEC including cell morphology, epithelial mRNA, protein expression profiles and genetic abnormalities. 5. A method according to any one of claims 2 to 4, wherein the pAEC or expanded pAEC are stably transfected to produce recombinant pAEC which express non-naturally occurring molecules. 6. A method according to claim 5, wherein the non-naturally occurring molecule is a reporter gene. 7. A method according to claim 6, wherein the reporter gene is GFP, luciferase, Î²- galactosidase, Î²-glucuronidase, alkaline phosphatase, chloramphenicol acetyltransferase, eCFP, eGFP or eYFP. 8. A method according to claim 6 or claim 7, wherein the reporter gene is eYFP. 9. An expression vector comprising a nucleic acid sequence encoding a reporter polypeptide operatively linked to a nucleic acid sequence encoding CFTR or a mutant thereof, wherein the expression vector facilitates expression of at least 150,000 copies of the CFTR polypeptide or mutant thereof on the cytoplasmic membrane of a cell. 10. An expression vector according to claim 9, wherein the reporter polypeptide is selected from the group consisting of GFP, luciferase, Î²-galactosidase, Î²-glucuronidase, alkaline phosphatase, chloramphenicol acetyltransferase, eCFP, eGFP, and eYFP. 11. A recombinant pAEC comprising an expression vector comprising a nucleic acid sequence encoding a reporter polypeptide operatively linked to a nucleic acid sequence encoding CFTR or a mutant thereof, wherein the expression vector facilitates expression of at least 150,000 copies of the CFTR polypeptide or a mutant thereof on the cytoplasmic membrane of the pAEC. 12. A method of screening for a therapeutic agent for the treatment of cystic fibrosis comprising: (i) culturing a pAEC optionally a recombinant pAEC; (ii) contacting said pAEC or recombinant pAEC with a putative therapeutic agent; and (iii) detecting the effect of the putative therapeutic agent on the pAEC compared to the pAEC before the putative therapeutic agent was contacted. 13. A method of screening for a therapeutic agent for the treatment of cystic fibrosis comprising: (i) culturing in vitro a recombinant pAEC comprising a nucleic acid sequence encoding CFTR or a mutant thereof under conditions suitable for expression of said nucleic acid sequence; (ii) contacting said pAEC with a putative therapeutic agent such that said agent interacts detectably with a region of said CFTR polypeptide or a mutant thereof; and (iii) detecting said interaction of said putative therapeutic agent with said CFTR polypeptide or a mutant thereof, thereby identifying said putative therapeutic agent as an agent capable of treating cystic fibrosis. 14. A kit for use in screening for therapeutic agents capable of treating cystic fibrosis or diseases associated with cystic fibrosis comprising: (i) expanded pAEC, optionally expanded recombinant pAEC; and (ii) instructions for use. 15. A kit according to claim 14 further comprising: (iii) positive and/or negative control agents. 16. A kit according to claim 15, wherein the positive control agent is an agent known to effect CFTR function. 17. A method according to claim 13, wherein the method of detecting said interaction of said putative therapeutic agent with said CFTR polypeptide or a mutant thereof is detection of second messenger activity. 18. A method according to claim 17, wherein the second messenger activity comprises a change in intracellular calcium levels, cAMP activity, and NFAT or CRE, driven beta- lactamase. 19. A method according to claim 17, wherein the reporter activity readout comprises detection of GFP, eYFP, luciferase, or a radio-labelled molecule. 20. A method according to any one of claims 17 to 19, wherein the method of screening includes high, throughput screening methods to detect said second messenger activity.
